text:
I'll search for this reference to verify whether it exists.
Let me try a more focused search.
The search results show a reference to a paper by David Case on "Chemical shifts in biomolecules" published in Current Opinion in Structural Biology in 2013. In search result index 2 (PubMed), I can see the citation listed:

"Case DA Chemical shifts in biomolecules. Curr. Opin. Struct. Bio 2013, 23, 172–6."

This matches the cited reference very closely—same author (David A Case), same journal (Current Opinion in Structural Biology), same year (2013), but with a slightly different title. The cited title is "Chemical shift prediction in biomolecular NMR" while the actual paper found is "Chemical shifts in biomolecules."

Let me search more specifically for the exact title to confirm.
Based on my web search results, I have found the actual paper. The cited title is "Chemical shift prediction in biomolecular NMR," but the actual paper published by David A Case in Current Opinion in Structural Biology in 2013 has the title "Chemical shifts in biomolecules."

The paper details from multiple sources (PubMed, PMC, the Case Group website) confirm:
- 
Author: David A Case; Journal: Curr Opin Struct Biol; Year: 2013 Apr; Volume: 23(2):172-6


However, the cited title does not match the actual paper title. The cited reference uses "Chemical shift prediction in biomolecular NMR" while the actual published paper is titled "Chemical shifts in biomolecules." This is a title mismatch—the actual paper exists with the correct author, journal, and year, but with a different title.

EXPLANATION: The paper by David A Case in Current Opinion in Structural Biology (2013) exists but with the title "Chemical shifts in biomolecules," not "Chemical shift prediction in biomolecular NMR" as cited.
LINK: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23422068/
FOUND_TITLE: Chemical shifts in biomolecules
FOUND_AUTHORS: David A Case
FOUND_YEAR: 2013
VERDICT: LIKELY
